title: Luscious Smoky Slovakian Pork Goulash
description: A hearty, comforting Slovakian dish featuring the rich flavors of Marsala wine and a hint of smokiness. This recipe is anti-inflammatory-friendly and best served as a main course or with some crusty bread for a satisfying snack.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the Marsala wine to the pot and cook until the liquid has reduced by half, about 2 minutes.
Add the browned pork back to the pot, along with the bay leaves, carrots, and potatoes.
Add enough water to cover the ingredients and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the goulash hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over egg noodles for a satisfying meal.
Let the goulash rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
